prompt:
Platform-eng ticket. Our Atlas project inventory job still authenticates with legacy Digest API keys and a hand-rolled pager that hits pageNum in a loop until it gets an empty page — security wants it on service accounts (OAuth 2.0), and it silently dropped a page last week. I want a proper Go client for the MongoDB Atlas Administration API v2: package atlasadmin (module go-atlas-oauth-projects, stdlib only, repo root). atlas_test.go is the acceptance contract and runs under -race.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official MongoDB documentation — at minimum the service-account token-generation page (exact token endpoint, headers, form body, response and error shapes, token lifetime) and the Atlas Admin API v2 reference for the list-projects operation plus the pagination/links and versioned-media-type conventions. Only first-party MongoDB pages count as sources (mongodb.com/docs, the official Atlas Admin API v2 reference, or MongoDB's own SDK repos). Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both protected. If the live docs disagree with the fixtures, flag the conflict in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Surface the tests compile against:
- New(Config{TokenURL, BaseURL, ClientID, ClientSecret string, HTTPClient *http.Client (nil => http.DefaultClient), Now func() time.Time (nil => time.Now)}) (*Client, error) — reject any empty TokenURL/BaseURL/ClientID/ClientSecret.
- (*Client).Projects(ctx context.Context) ([]Project, error) with Project{ID, Name, OrgID, Created string, ClusterCount int64}.
- Token handling: exchange exactly per the documented client-credentials contract (the docs say which header carries the client ID/secret, the exact content type, and the exact form body). Cache the token and reuse it across calls and pages; re-exchange only when the injected clock says the token is within 60s of its expires_in, when a request comes back 401 (drop the cached token, exchange once, retry that request once — never loop), or on first use. Concurrent callers must share ONE in-flight exchange (the tests hammer this under -race; the real endpoint is rate limited to 10/min).
- Credential separation: the Basic credential goes ONLY to the token host; the Bearer token goes ONLY to the API host. Never send Bearer to the token endpoint, never send Basic to the API host, and refuse (without fetching) any pagination href that points off the API host. No error string may contain the secret or a token.
- Projects paging: GET the documented v2 groups collection with the documented dated Accept media type, then follow the response links exactly as the docs describe (which rel means more pages, how the last page is signalled). Preserve server order across pages. An empty collection is a success with zero projects.
- Errors: non-2xx API responses decode the documented Atlas error envelope into *APIError{StatusCode int, ErrorCode, Detail, Reason string}; a failed token exchange decodes the documented OAuth error body into *TokenError{Code, Description string}. Both implement error. Context cancellation must surface an error wrapping context.Canceled.

Acceptance: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... — it runs loopback token/API hosts plus a decoy host that must never be contacted; no real Atlas, no credentials, no sleeps. atlas_test.go and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: Atlas Administration API v2 (GET /api/atlas/v2/groups, dated media type 2023-01-01) with OAuth 2.0 service-account token exchange

official_sources.title: Generate Service Account Token - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: token endpoint POST https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/oauth/token; headers Authorization: Basic base64(clientId:secret), Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded, Accept application/json; body grant_type=client_credentials; success shape {access_token, expires_in: 3600, token_type: Bearer}; OAuth error shape {error, error_description} with codes invalid_client/invalid_request/unsupported_grant_type/rate_limited; token endpoint limited to 10 requests per minute

official_sources.title: Service Accounts Overview - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: service accounts use OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow; client ID + rotatable secret function as username/password only for creating access tokens; tokens live 1 hour (3600s) and are reusable within it

official_sources.title: Return All Projects | Atlas Admin API v2 documentation
official_sources.used_for: GET /api/atlas/v2/groups; query params pageNum (default 1), itemsPerPage (default 100, max 500), includeCount (default true); dated media type application/vnd.atlas.2023-01-01+json is the current stable version for this resource; response {results, links[{href,rel}], totalCount}; RateLimit-Limit/RateLimit-Remaining headers; service-account auth supported

official_sources.title: Atlas Administration API Reference - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: list envelope {totalCount, results, links}; link relations previous (omitted on first page), next (omitted on last page), self (always); links live in the response body; empty result set is 200 with empty results, not 404; error document {detail, error, errorCode, parameters, reason} with example RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND

official_sources.title: Versioned Atlas Administration API Overview - Atlas - MongoDB Docs
official_sources.used_for: Accept: application/vnd.atlas.<yyyy-mm-dd>+json format; 406 Not Acceptable when the Accept header omits or names a nonexistent resource version; resource-level versioning lifecycle

official_sources.title: MongoDB Atlas Administration API 2.0 — official OpenAPI specification (latest resource versions)
official_sources.used_for: server url https://cloud.mongodb.com; listGroups 200 media type application/vnd.atlas.2023-01-01+json; PaginatedAtlasGroupView {links[Link{href,rel}], results[Group], totalCount}; Group required fields clusterCount/created/name/orgId, id pattern ^([a-f0-9]{24})$; ApiError {badRequestDetail, detail, error:int32, errorCode, parameters[], reason} served as application/json; spec info block confirms the OAuth token endpoint and 1-hour bearer tokens

research.language_rationale: The platform tooling this inventory job joins is Go. MongoDB's official Admin SDK for Go (mongodb/atlas-sdk-go) is a generated thin wrapper over exactly these documented REST endpoints and delegates service-account auth to a standard OAuth2 client-credentials round trip, so a zero-dependency stdlib net/http implementation against the documented wire contract is a credible, idiomatic integration surface — and it keeps the token-host/API-host credential separation explicit instead of hidden inside a transport.
